name: Resin Business OS — Gmail → inbound email webhook

nodes.name: Gmail — new message
nodes.notes: Attach Gmail OAuth2 (gmailOAuth2). Adjust filters.q (Gmail search) to match your traffic.

nodes.name: POST → Resin OS inbound
nodes.notes: Default URL points at Railway production. If your Railway hostname differs, edit only the host; keep path /api/webhooks/email/inbound.

parameters.content:
## Gmail → Resin OS (inbound)

### App `.env`
- `EMAIL_INBOUND_SIGNING_SECRET` **or** `N8N_WEBHOOK_SIGNING_SECRET` (same value you put in n8n).

### n8n Cloud
1. **Settings → Variables:** create `RESIN_EMAIL_WEBHOOK_SECRET` (same as app secret).
2. If Code errors on `require('crypto')`: enable Node.js modules for Code (workflow / instance security settings) or upgrade n8n.
3. **HTTP Request → URL:** must be **only** the https URL (no extra text). This template uses **`https://resin-business-os-production.up.railway.app/api/webhooks/email/inbound`** — change the host if your Railway URL is different.

### If n8n shows “Update parameter” warnings
- **Gmail search `q`:** real Gmail query (template: `in:inbox newer_than:7d`).
- **HTTP URL:** must match your live app and stay on path `/api/webhooks/email/inbound`.

### If you still see errors before run
- Gmail trigger: attach **Gmail OAuth2**.
- Code: `RESIN_EMAIL_WEBHOOK_SECRET` variable must exist on n8n Cloud.
